title: Fix app loading failure with robust error handling and auth initialization

requirements.text: Add a top-level error boundary in App.tsx that catches JavaScript errors during initial render and displays a fallback UI instead of a blank screen.
acceptanceCriteria: A React ErrorBoundary wraps the root router/layout so that uncaught render errors show a readable fallback message instead of a blank page.
The fallback UI includes a 'Reload' button that calls window.location.reload().

requirements.text: Guard the App router initialization so that the authentication state from InternetIdentityProvider is fully resolved before any route component renders, showing a loading spinner in the meantime.
acceptanceCriteria: While the AuthClient is initializing, the app displays a centered loading spinner rather than attempting to render routes.
Once auth state is resolved, the correct route (landing or vault) renders without a blank or broken state.

requirements.text: Make the LandingPage 'Get Started' button resilient to auth initialization errors: wrap the login call in a try/catch, display a user-friendly error toast on failure, and never leave the button in a permanently disabled or silent-fail state.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Get Started' while auth is still initializing shows a loading state on the button.
If the Internet Identity login call throws, an error toast is displayed and the button returns to its default state.
Authenticated users visiting the landing page are immediately redirected to /vault.

requirements.text: Wrap the useActor hook's actor creation in a try/catch so that a failure to instantiate the backend actor does not crash the app; instead, queries that depend on the actor should gracefully return an empty/error state.
acceptanceCriteria: If actor creation fails, the hook returns null/undefined without throwing.
React Query hooks that use the actor handle a null actor by skipping the fetch and not crashing the component tree.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend Motoko actor or data schema.
nonGoals: Adding new vault features or UI pages.
Changing the visual design or theme.
